name: El Mollet and Cova de les Ferreries Loop
name_original: Paraje Natural El Mollet — Circular desde Borriol por la Cova de Les Ferreries
description: This moderate 20.2-mile mountain bike route near Borriol explores the Paratge Natural Municipal El Mollet, featuring the historic Cova de Le
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Borriol offer a diverse range of experiences across varied landscapes. The region is characterized by rugged mountainous terrain, valleys, and forests, providing both scenic backdrops and challenging ascents. An extensive network of routes caters to various skill levels, from easy rides to technical paths. This area in Castellón is well-suited for mountain biking due to its natural features and diverse trail options. Best mountain bike trails around Borriol The most popular mountain bike trail is Paraje Natural El Mollet — Circular desde Borriol por la Cova de Les Ferreries, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural features of Paraje Natural El Mollet. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Leprosos S1 Ascent – La Torreta Quarry loop from Borriol, a difficult 12.8 miles (20.6 km) path. This route features technical sections and significant elevation gain through rugged terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Font de Paret – Serrería Trail loop from Borriol, a 17.4 miles (27.9 km) trail leading through valleys and forests, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Borriol is defined by its mountains, valleys, and forests, including the prominent Paraje Natural El Mollet. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, accommodating different ability levels. The routes in Borriol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Borriol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Borriol?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Borriol, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 4 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 19 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Borriol?
A: Yes, Borriol offers several easier routes suitable for families or beginners. There are 4 easy trails available, providing a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Borriol?
A: Borriol's mountain bike trails feature diverse terrain, including rugged mountainous areas, valleys, and forests. You'll find everything from scenic backdrops to challenging ascents and technical sections, with routes categorized by difficulty from S0-S1 (easy) to S3-S6 (very hard).

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Borriol?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Borriol are circular. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932039367/paraje-natural-el-mollet-circular-desde-borriol-por-la-cova-de-les-ferreries" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paraje Natural El Mollet — Circular desde Borriol por la Cova de Les Ferreries&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option exploring natural features. Another challenging circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34516581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leprosos S1 Ascent – La Torreta Quarry loop from Borriol&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while mountain biking in Borriol?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the prominent Paraje Natural El Mollet, which is featured in several routes. Other natural attractions nearby include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1323360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bartolo Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1062550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocas de Mallassén&lt;/a&gt;. The Vía Verde del Mar, a cycleway nearby, also offers a unique blend of sea and mountain scenery.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the trails, Borriol has a rich history. You can find remains of a medieval castle offering panoramic views, the 18th-century Parroquia de San Bartolomé, and ancient Iberian and Argaric settlements. The Joquera route, for instance, combines hiking with historical exploration, passing ancient cave paintings and traditional buildings.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Borriol?
A: The mountain bike trails in Borriol are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. Many riders praise the varied terrain and the beautiful natural landscapes, from rugged mountains to serene valleys and forests.

Q: Are there options for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Absolutely. Borriol is known for its challenging routes, with 19 difficult trails available. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39554558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de Paret – Serrería Trail loop from Borriol&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrería Trail – Cami de Sant Miquel loop from Borriol&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and technical sections, designed for experienced riders.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain biking areas in Borriol?
A: While Borriol is well-suited for outdoor activities, specific public transport access directly to trailheads can vary. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the starting points, as many routes begin directly from Borriol itself.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Borriol?
A: Borriol generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn typically provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer a good mix of scenery and challenge?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932039367/paraje-natural-el-mollet-circular-desde-borriol-por-la-cova-de-les-ferreries" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paraje Natural El Mollet — Circular desde Borriol por la Cova de Les Ferreries&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate option that combines scenic views of the natural park with a good level of challenge. For a more difficult option with stunning landscapes, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32103177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de Paret – Singletrack Trail loop from el Poble Nou&lt;/a&gt;.

Somewhat lost and hidden within the forest, it is a curious building that also has a water source. A good place to rest and recover energy.

Although the cave is quite hidden, it is easy to reach it by following the signs on the way. Perhaps used in the past as a refuge, today it is an adventure destination that can be reached by a well-surfaced path.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Paraje Natural El Mollet mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near Borriol. You should be able to find parking within the town of Borriol, which serves as the trailhead for this circular route. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors accessing the natural park.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While some sections might be beginner-friendly (STS S0-S1), riders should have good fitness levels and be prepared for varied terrain, including demanding inclines and descents. Some parts may require intermediate skills (STS S2) or even pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of the Borriol region. You'll encounter wide dirt tracks, some with demanding ramps up to a 10% gradient, as well as singletrack and doubletrack sections. Expect rocky parts, roots, and flowy descents. The route also passes through agricultural areas with orange and avocado groves, which may offer smoother sections.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass several points of interest. Key highlights include the historic Cueva de las Ferrerías, an old iron mine, the Sant Miquel Hermitage, and the Pou de Mollet. You'll also see old corrals and traverse scenic ravines and mountainous formations like Tossal Roig.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 32.5-kilometer mountain biking loop is just over 3 hours, assuming a steady pace. Your actual time may vary based on your fitness level, breaks, and how much time you spend exploring the highlights.

faqs.question: Is the Paraje Natural El Mollet — Circular desde Borriol por la Cova de Les Ferreries trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed in natural parks like Paratge Natural Municipal El Mollet, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for mountain biking in Paratge Natural Municipal El Mollet?
faqs.answer: As of current information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking this route within the Paratge Natural Municipal El Mollet. However, always respect local regulations and signage within the natural park.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride along parts of El Camí IT6TR1 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, Sender de la Lluna Plena, Almenara - les Coves de Vinromà, Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana, and Castellón de la Plana - Borriol, among others.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or accommodation options near the trailhead in Borriol?
faqs.answer: Yes, Borriol is a town with amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in Borriol itself, making it convenient for pre-ride preparations or post-ride relaxation and meals.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate mountain bike ride, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, a repair kit for your bike, and a fully charged phone. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunnier months.
